p_steps = max(1, total_steps // 10)\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(current_step: int) -> float:\\n        step_idx = current_step + 1\\n        if step_idx <= warmup_steps:\\n            return step_idx / warmup_steps\\n        progress = (step_idx - warmup_steps) / max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps)\\n        return 0.1 + 0.9 * 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"With only 256 fixed training steps, the baseline underuses the budget by training at a small constant learning rate.
